pr.title: Match and improve hsd_3AA7, quatlib, and ftCo functions
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched seven previously non-100% functions and improved thirteen more across sysdolphin baselib CARD/save handling, quaternion matrix conversion, and one common fighter shouldered damage routine. The largest change was a major reshape of src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3AA7.c, especially the CARD command dispatcher hsd_803AAA48, using more explicit CardCmd/CardState access, volatile globals, reordered switch cases, exact retry/async paths, and local lifetime adjustments. The PR also raised MatToQuat from 95.55% to 99.92% by changing stack/local layout and expression reuse, and matched ftCo_8009C744 by using PAD_STACK, a direct victim_gobj access, and restoring a real ftColl_80078710 call.

key_files.role: Primary decomp work; large CARD/save command dispatcher and helper matching improvements, including new 100% matches for fn_803ACF30, fn_803ADE4C, fn_803B21E8, fn_803B1F78, hsd_803B2374, and fn_803AC168.
MatToQuat improvement from 95.55% to 99.92% by reshaping local declarations, scale variable reuse, and arithmetic expression structure; todo updated to note only an 8-byte stack-frame issue remains.
ftCo_8009C744 100% match; replaced dummy stack padding with PAD_STACK(8), adjusted victim object use, and restored ftColl_80078710 call.

what_changed: Added or reshaped 1653 lines and removed 865 lines in hsd_3AA7.c, mostly around CARD command queue handling and related helper functions.
Declared hsd_804D7980 as volatile s32, aligning it with nearby volatile global queue/index state.

In hsd_803AAA48, changed command dispatch to use cmd->type and switch ((u32) type), reordered/expanded cases, and repeatedly accessed cmd->state rather than keeping a separate CardState* local in many paths.

Expanded CARD async read/write/create/status code paths with explicit 10-iteration retry loops around CARDFastOpen, CARDReadAsync, CARDWriteAsync, CARDCreateAsync, CARDSetStatusAsync, CARDClose, and related APIs.

Reworked save-block/header builder paths in hsd_3AA7.c: header offset/rem calculations, memcpy/memset writes, digest initialization, hsd_803B2FA0 calls, and transferred-byte bookkeeping were made more source-like for matching.

Matched ftCo_8009C744 by adding PAD_STACK(8), calling ftCo_800DC920(fp->victim_gobj, gobj), and replacing a commented ftColl_80078710 placeholder with ftColl_80078710(vic_gobj, gobj, &fp->dmg.facing_dir_1).

Improved MatToQuat by moving q3 before nxt/lenCol, replacing sc0/sc1/sc2 temporaries with direct normalized matrix expressions plus one scale variable, and preserving the remaining todo as a stack-frame-size issue.
New 100% matches reported by decomp.dev: ftCo_8009C744, fn_803ACF30, fn_803ADE4C, fn_803B21E8, fn_803B1F78, hsd_803B2374, and fn_803AC168.

Other reported improvements included hsd_803AAA48 from 28.60% to 77.68%, fn_803B1338 from 37.08% to 84.80%, fn_803AF3F0 from 48.20% to 83.22%, fn_803AE7F8 from 41.72% to 73.77%, and MatToQuat from 95.55% to 99.92%.
smart_moves: Used PAD_STACK(8) in ftCo_8009C744 instead of an anonymous zeroed byte array, making the stack-match intent explicit and reusable.

Restored a real collision helper call, ftColl_80078710(vic_gobj, gobj, &fp->dmg.facing_dir_1), rather than leaving a commented placeholder; this both improved correctness of the decomp and unlocked the 100% match.

For hsd_803AAA48, preserved volatile/global access patterns instead of over-caching queue state in locals; this is visible in the volatile hsd_804D7980 declaration and repeated cmd->state / hsd_804D1138 accesses.
Changed boolean-looking expressions such as hsd_804D799C == 1 into explicit if/else assignments, a common CodeWarrior matching tactic when the compiler emits branchy boolean materialization.
Reordered switch cases and case bodies to reflect emitted control-flow rather than source-level neatness, especially around command types 1, 2, 5, 6, 10, 11, 15, and 16.
In MatToQuat, reduced long-lived scalar temporaries sc0/sc1/sc2 and used direct normalized matrix element expressions, improving register/stack lifetime behavior while keeping the algorithm readable.
Used decomp.dev and local build verification to quantify both exact matches and partial improvements, making a broad reshaping PR reviewable despite large diff size.
decomp_lessons: When matching large command dispatchers, the order of switch cases, continue/return placement, and when globals are reloaded can matter as much as the high-level behavior.
Do not assume helper pointer locals improve matching: replacing a CardState* state local with repeated cmd->state accesses can better match original codegen and local lifetimes.
Volatile annotations on queue indices or shared state can be necessary for matching interrupt-driven or async CARD code because the compiler must preserve reload/store behavior.
For CodeWarrior boolean materialization, an explicit if/else assigning 1 or 0 may match where a compact comparison expression does not.
Use PAD_STACK for intentional stack padding so future decomp work can distinguish required ABI/codegen padding from unknown data or fake locals.
Quaternion/matrix math matching can hinge on temporary lifetime and declaration order; direct recomputation may outperform named temporaries if the original compiler did not keep those values alive.
For save/CARD code, async APIs often need exact retry-loop shape, interrupt disable/restore placement, busy flag writes, and close-on-error cleanup structure to match.
Leaving a function at 99.9% with a precise todo, such as an 8-byte stack-frame mismatch, is useful evidence for later focused cleanup.
naming_conventions: Fighter object variables followed existing short decomp style: gobj, fp, vic_gobj, and vic_fp.

CARD/save structs in hsd_3AA7.c used descriptive recovered names CardOpStatus, CardBufEntry, CardCmd, and CardState while retaining offset-like fields such as x10, x18, x20 where semantics are not fully recovered.
Unmatched or partially understood baselib functions retained address-derived names such as hsd_803AAA48, fn_803ACF30, fn_803ADE4C, fn_803B21E8, fn_803B1F78, hsd_803B2374, and fn_803AC168.
MatToQuat kept the established library-style function name and used generic math locals q3, nxt, lenCol, scale, tr, i, j, and k.
Stack-padding intent was expressed with PAD_STACK(8), not a throwaway underscore byte array.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use volatile on shared globals to force reloads/stores in async or interrupt-sensitive code.
Prefer explicit branchy forms for simple booleans when matching CodeWarrior output.
Cast switch discriminants, e.g. switch ((u32) type), when signedness changes jump-table or compare generation.
Reorder switch case source bodies to match the binary control-flow layout instead of logical numeric order.
Control local variable lifetimes by removing helper locals and accessing nested fields directly, such as cmd->state->file_info.
Match stack frames with PAD_STACK when a required unused stack slot is known.
For math functions, declaration order and temporary reuse can change stack allocation; MatToQuat improved by moving q3 and consolidating sc0/sc1/sc2 into scale plus direct expressions.
Preserve exact async operation patterns: retry loops up to 10, OSDisableInterrupts/OSRestoreInterrupts boundaries, hsd_804D799C busy flag timing, and CARDClose cleanup on negative returns.
Small arithmetic reordering can matter, as shown by changing a loop bound expression to (0x2F + state->x24 + state->x8) / state->x8.
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the PR slice.
The PR received an APPROVED review from jurrejelle on 2026-06-05T17:20:56Z.
decomp.dev reported +2332 matched bytes and 70.21% total matched code for GALE01, with seven new matches and thirteen improved unmatched items.
The PR body recorded verification with python configure.py --require-protos and ninja, plus GALE01 report confirmation for the listed 100.0% functions.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish MatToQuat's remaining 8-byte stack-frame mismatch.
Find later hsd_3AA7.c PRs that continue hsd_803AAA48, fn_803B1338, fn_803AF3F0, or fn_803AE7F8 toward 100%.
Search for other PRs using PAD_STACK to replace dummy byte arrays for fighter function matches.
Search for volatile queue/index globals in CARD or async baselib code to reuse the hsd_804D7980 tactic.
Compare later CardCmd/CardState field naming improvements for x0/x4/x8/x10/x18/x20/x24 and digest/icon fields.
